* **Icons:** On the lower portion of the top panel, there are two small white icons: one representing a human head with sound waves (likely indicating wireless input/reception) and another representing headphones (indicating audio output). * **Buttons:** On the right side of the device, there is a button marked with a double arrow symbol (`< >`), which typically indicates volume control or navigation. A small, circular feature is visible on the bottom front edge, which may be a button or an LED indicator. * **Ports:** A USB-C port is located on the bottom front edge, presumably for charging the device. A thick, black cable is connected to the top of the main unit through two ribbed strain reliefs. The cable extends upward and forms a large loop that terminates in a wider, textured black module. This configuration suggests the device is part of a wearable system, such as a neckband or a wireless audio receiver designed to be worn around the neck. The overall purpose of the system appears to be a portable, wearable wireless audio solution, potentially for personal hearing assistance or high-quality wireless audio streaming.]

figures.description:
This image shows a matte black electronic device, identified by the text "**iCube II**" printed in light grey on its top surface. The device is rectangular with rounded corners and is set against a plain white background.

The main unit features several functional markings and interfaces:
* **Icons:** On the lower portion of the top panel, there are two small white icons: one representing a human head with sound waves (likely indicating wireless input/reception) and another representing headphones (indicating audio output).
* **Buttons:** On the right side of the device, there is a button marked with a double arrow symbol (`< >`), which typically indicates volume control or navigation. A small, circular feature is visible on the bottom front edge, which may be a button or an LED indicator.
* **Ports:** A USB-C port is located on the bottom front edge, presumably for charging the device.

A thick, black cable is connected to the top of the main unit through two ribbed strain reliefs. The cable extends upward and forms a large loop that terminates in a wider, textured black module. This configuration suggests the device is part of a wearable system, such as a neckband or a wireless audio receiver designed to be worn around the neck.

The overall purpose of the system appears to be a portable, wearable wireless audio solution, potentially for personal hearing assistance or high-quality wireless audio streaming.

This image shows a matte black electronic device, identified by the text "**iCube II**" on its top surface. The device appears to be a wireless audio transmitter or receiver, likely designed for hearing assistance or specialized audio connectivity.

**Main Unit:**
The central component is a compact, rectangular unit with heavily rounded corners. Its features include:
* **Top Surface:** The label "**iCube II**" is printed in a light gray font. Below the text are two small white icons resembling headphones or ears, separated by two faint horizontal dashes.
* **Side Controls:** On the right side of the unit, a button is visible, marked with two arrow-like symbols (`< >`) pointing towards each other.
* **Bottom Edge:** A silver-colored USB-C port is located on the bottom front edge, presumably for charging or data transfer.

**Cable and Loop:**
A black, flexible cable extends from the top of the main unit. The cable forms a large loop that includes a secondary black component positioned in the center of the loop. This central component is elongated and features ribbed strain relief at both ends where it connects to the cable. The cable also has ribbed reinforcement where it enters the main unit.

**Overall Purpose:**
Based on the "iCube II" branding and the ear icons, the system is an audio-related device. The design, with the cable loop, suggests it may function as an induction loop system or a specialized wireless audio bridge meant to be worn or placed near a user.

The purpose of the page is to instruct a user on how to select, attach, and remove regional power adapters for a power supply unit. **Components and Selection:**
At the top, the text "Select the adapter for your country" is followed by two line drawings of power plugs:
* **US:** A two-prong flat-pin adapter. * **EU:** A two-prong round-pin adapter. **Assembly Instructions:**
The manual provides a two-step process for attaching an adapter:
1. "Insert the top round end of the adapter into the round corner of the power supply."
2. "Click the tail of the adapter in to lock it into place."

A corresponding diagram illustrates this. A large horizontal arrow labeled "**1.**" shows the adapter being pushed into the power supply, and a curved arrow labeled "**2.**" shows the bottom portion of the adapter being rotated or clicked into a locked position. **Removal Instructions:**
A second two-step process explains how to detach the adapter:
1. "To remove, pull back the tab on the power supply."
2. "Pull the adapter upwards to remove it."

The second diagram illustrates this process. A downward-pointing arrow labeled "**1.**" indicates pressing a small release tab on the power supply body, and an upward-pointing arrow labeled "**2.**" shows the adapter being pulled away from the unit.]

This image is a page from a user manual, specifically page 9, containing instructional information regarding the "iCube II" device. The layout consists of several text-based informational notes, each housed in a light grey box and preceded by an information icon (a lowercase "i" inside a circle).

The content is organized as follows:

*   **Optional Accessory:** A single note at the top states, "iCube II desktop stand is available as an option."
*   **Battery notes section:** Following the heading "Battery notes," there are four specific instructions:
    *   **Initial Charging:** Users are instructed to leave the iCube II to charge for at least three hours when charging for the first time, even if the middle indicator light turns green sooner.
    *   **Charging Duration:** Charging a depleted battery takes approximately 2 hours. The note also clarifies that it is safe to leave the device connected for longer periods, even when not in use.
    *   **Performance Optimization:** It states that a new battery achieves full performance only after completing three full charge and discharge cycles.
    *   **Battery Lifespan and Support:** The note mentions the battery can be charged several hundred times. It advises users to contact their local Phonak representative if the operating time of the device reduces significantly.

The overall purpose of the page is to provide essential guidance on the maintenance, charging protocols, and expected lifecycle of the iCube II battery to ensure optimal device performance.

This is a page from a technical user manual, specifically page 10, under the heading "**4. Getting started**." The document provides instructions for a device called the "**iCube II**."

The page is organized into three numbered sections:

*   **4.3 Operating time:** This section states that the iCube II will operate for up to "twelve hours of continuous fitting" when fully charged.
*   **4.4 Switching iCube II on and off:** This section explains power operation. It contains text instructing the user to "Slide the switch as shown to switch iCube II on or off." section: 5. Connecting iCube II to your computer
chunk_title: 5. Connecting iCube II to your computer (part 1)
content:
iCube II is shipped with a pre-paired wireless
USB adapter to ensure a quick and easy
set-up of the wireless connection with your
fitting PC. iCube II may also be connected with any Bluetooth
capable PC if a USB socket is not available at all or via a
USB cable if a wireless connection is not possible. 5.1 Connecting wirelessly
Automatic connection
To connect iCube II with the fitting PC make sure
that Phonak Target 4.0 and newer is installed. Plug the
USB adapter into a free USB port on your fitting PC. Make sure the USB adapter is plugged in a USB
port on the fitting PC. Do not use a USB extension
cable to connect it. --- Page 12 ---
5. Connecting iCube II to your computer
Switch iCube II on and the connection will be
established automatically as soon as Phonak Target is
started. The USB adapter is pre-paired with iCube II . After
replacement of either the adapter or iCube II ,
a manual pairing is required as described below. Manual pairing
Switch iCube II on and press the pairing button
for 2 seconds to start the pairing mode. When in pairing mode, all indicator lights will rapidly
blink back and forth. In Phonak Target, go to Settings ,
select Fitting device and check Enable iCube II . Go to iCube II via USB adapter and start the Pairing
assistant . It will guide you through the pairing process. A manual pairing is also required after a reset to
factory settings. --- Page 13 ---

*   **Instructional Text:** "To connect iCube II via USB cable, insert the mini-USB plug into iCube II and the other end into a free USB port on the fitting PC.

This image is a page from an instructional manual, specifically page 14, titled "**6. Using iCube II**". The purpose of the page is to provide instructions on preparing and wearing a device called the iCube II.

### **Text Content**
The page contains the following text:
*   **Heading:** 6. Using iCube II
*   **Introductory Paragraph:** "Before using iCube II, make sure the battery is sufficiently charged. If the middle indicator light is orange, please charge iCube II. Make sure that iCube II is selected and shown as the fitting device in Phonak Target."
*   **Subheading:** 6.1 Wearing iCube II
*   **Instruction 1:** "Make sure both ends of the neckloop are firmly plugged into the iCube II."
*   **Instruction 2:** "Open the neckloop by unplugging the connector in the middle of the neckloop and place iCube II around your client's neck."
*   **Instruction 3:** "Close the neckloop to allow the built-in antenna to operate correctly."
*   **Page Number:** 14 (located at the bottom left).

### **Visual Diagrams and Relationships**
The page includes two line-art diagrams that correspond to the written instructions:

1.  **Top Diagram:** This diagram illustrates the first instruction regarding plugging in the neckloop. It shows the top portion of the iCube II device with two green-colored wire connectors being inserted into the device. Two green arrows point downwards, indicating the action of plugging the connectors into the device.
2.  **Bottom Diagram:** This diagram illustrates the second instruction about opening the neckloop. It shows a U-shaped neckloop with a central connection point. The connection is shown as split, with two green arrows pointing outwards from the center, indicating that the loop should be opened by pulling the two ends of the connector apart.

The overall layout uses black text on a white background, with green accents used for instructional arrows and specific components in the diagrams to draw the reader's attention to the required actions.

This image is a page from an instructional manual, specifically page 14, titled "**6. Using iCube II**". The purpose of the page is to provide instructions on preparing and wearing a device called the iCube II.

### **Text Content**
The page contains the following text:
*   **Heading:** 6. Using iCube II
*   **Introductory Paragraph:** "Before using iCube II, make sure the battery is sufficiently charged. If the middle indicator light is orange, please charge iCube II. Make sure that iCube II is selected and shown as the fitting device in Phonak Target."
*   **Subheading:** 6.1 Wearing iCube II
*   **Instruction 1:** "Make sure both ends of the neckloop are firmly plugged into the iCube II."
*   **Instruction 2:** "Open the neckloop by unplugging the connector in the middle of the neckloop and place iCube II around your client's neck."
*   **Instruction 3:** "Close the neckloop to allow the built-in antenna to operate correctly."
*   **Page Number:** 14 (located at the bottom left).

### **Visual Diagrams and Relationships**
The page includes two line-art diagrams that correspond to the written instructions:

1.  **Top Diagram:** This diagram illustrates the first instruction regarding plugging in the neckloop. It shows the top portion of the iCube II device with two green-colored wire connectors being inserted into the device. Two green arrows point downwards, indicating the action of plugging the connectors into the device.
2.  **Bottom Diagram:** This diagram illustrates the second instruction about opening the neckloop. It shows a U-shaped neckloop with a central connection point. The connection is shown as split, with two green arrows pointing outwards from the center, indicating that the loop should be opened by pulling the two ends of the connector apart.

The overall layout uses black text on a white background, with green accents used for instructional arrows and specific components in the diagrams to draw the reader's attention to the required actions.

section: 5. Connecting iCube II to your computer
chunk_title: 5. Connecting iCube II to your computer
content:
iCube II is shipped with a pre-paired wireless
USB adapter to ensure a quick and easy
set-up of the wireless connection with your
fitting PC.
iCube II may also be connected with any Bluetooth
capable PC if a USB socket is not available at all or via a
USB cable if a wireless connection is not possible.
5.1 Connecting wirelessly
Automatic connection
To connect iCube II with the fitting PC make sure
that Phonak Target 4.0 and newer is installed. Plug the
USB adapter into a free USB port on your fitting PC.
Make sure the USB adapter is plugged in a USB
port on the fitting PC. Do not use a USB extension
cable to connect it.
--- Page 12 ---
5. Connecting iCube II to your computer
Switch iCube II on and the connection will be
established automatically as soon as Phonak Target is
started.
The USB adapter is pre-paired with iCube II . After
replacement of either the adapter or iCube II ,
a manual pairing is required as described below.
Manual pairing
Switch iCube II on and press the pairing button
for 2 seconds to start the pairing mode.
When in pairing mode, all indicator lights will rapidly
blink back and forth. In Phonak Target, go to Settings ,
select Fitting device and check Enable iCube II .
Go to iCube II via USB adapter and start the Pairing
assistant . It will guide you through the pairing process.
A manual pairing is also required after a reset to
factory settings.
--- Page 13 ---

6.2	Detecting and programming hearing instruments
For a wireless fitting, the assignment of the left/right
hearing aid is done in Phonak Target. Make sure that
iCube II is selected as fitting device and click Connect . The detected devices will be shown to assign (or reassign)
each hearing instrument to the desired side (left or right). Click Continue . Follow the instructions on the screen to finish the
detection. --- Page 16 ---

(Note: the word "instruments" is misspelled as "instuments" in the original text).

The page features two line-art diagrams:

* **Top Diagram (enclosed in a green circle):** This illustrates the specific setup described in the text. It shows two in-the-ear hearing instruments positioned inside a large loop formed by a neckloop (a cable with connectors).
* **Bottom Diagram:** This depicts the **iCube II** device. It is a rectangular, handheld unit with the label "**iCube II**" on its face. The diagram shows two cables extending from the top of the device and features a small display area and two buttons at the bottom.

**Relationships and Purpose:**
The overall purpose of the page is to instruct a professional on how to set up the iCube II system for "desk programming." The iCube II device connects to the hearing instruments via the neckloop. By placing the hearing instruments within the loop of the neckloop on a flat surface, the user can prepare and program the devices before a patient fitting session.

This image is a page from a technical manual, specifically section "7. Indicator lights" (page 17). The purpose of the page is to explain the meaning of the various light patterns on the "iCube II" device, which uses three indicator lights to communicate battery, connection, and fitting status.

The page consists of an introductory sentence followed by four rows, each pairing a textual description of a light's behavior with a visual diagram and a status label. Each diagram shows a rounded rectangular panel containing three horizontal light slots and two small icons below them.

The four states described are:

1.  **Charging:** The text on the left states, "Middle indicator remains solid orange." The diagram shows an orange light in the center slot, and the label on the right reads "Charging."
2.  **Charging complete:** The text on the left states, "Middle indicator remains solid green." The diagram shows a green light in the center slot, and the label on the right reads "Charging complete."
3.  **iCube II starting up:** The text on the left states, "Middle indicator is solid green for 2 s." The diagram shows a green light in the center slot with the annotation "2 sec" above it. The label on the right reads "iCube II starting up."
4.  **iCube II switching off:** The text on the left states, "Middle indicator slowly blinking 3 times." The diagram shows the center slot with concentric green arcs to represent blinking, with the annotation "3 x" above it. The label on the right reads "iCube II switching off."

The following pages contain important safety
information. Hazard warnings
Phonak devices may generate magnetic fields. Tests
that Phonak conducted showed that no interfe­
rences with the implantable devices were caused
that would impact the safe and effective use of the
implantable devices. Users of implantable devices
should contact their doctor and/or the manufacturer
of the implantable device before they start using a
Phonak device. Stop using the Phonak device if any
influence of the Phonak device on the implantable
device is experienced and contact the manufacturer
of the implantable device for advice. Keep this device out of reach of children under
3 years. Changes or modifications to the device that were
not explicitly approved by Phonak are not permitted. Phonak is not responsible for situations resulting
from a modified device. --- Page 33 ---
Opening the device might damage it. If problems
occur which cannot be resolved by following the
remedy guidelines in the troubleshooting section
of this user guide, consult your local Phonak
representative. This product has an embedded, non-replaceable
battery. Do not attempt to open the product or remove
the battery as this may cause injury and damage the
device. Dispose of electrical components in accordance with
your local regulations by Phonak AG. Only use accessories approved by Phonak AG, to
avoid any electrical shock. Using the device, its connectors or cables in any way
contradictory to their intended purpose as described
in this user guide (e.g. wearing the USB cable around
the neck) can cause injury. When operating machinery, ensure that no parts get
caught in the machine. --- Page 34 ---
12. Important safety information
The mini-USB port is to be used for the described
purpose only. For safety reasons, only use chargers supplied by
Phonak or stabilized chargers with a rating of 5VDC,
min. 500 mA. Caution: electric shock. Do not insert plug alone into
electrical outlets. Do not use the device in explosive areas (mines or
industrial areas with danger of explosions, oxygen
rich environments or areas where flammable
anesthetics are handled) or where electronic
equipment is prohibited.

content:
Do not use the device in explosive areas (mines or
industrial areas with danger of explosions, oxygen
rich environments or areas where flammable
anesthetics are handled) or where electronic
equipment is prohibited. --- Page 35 ---
Information on product safety
Protect the device from excessive moisture (bathing,
swimming), heat (radiator, car dashboard) or direct
skin contact when sweating. Do not drop the device. Dropping it onto a hard
surface can damage the device. Do not disconnect the neckloop while the device is
transmitting signals to the hearing aids. Protect the device connectors, plugs, charging slot
and power supply from dirt and debris. Clean the device using a damp cloth. Never use
household cleaning products (washing powder, soap,
etc.) or alcohol to clean the device. Never use a
microwave or other heating devices to dry the device. --- Page 36 ---
12. Important safety information
The digitally-coded, inductive transmission
technology used in the device is highly reliable and
experiences virtually no interference from other
devices. It should be noted, however, that when
operating the hearing system near computer
equipment, larger electronic installation or other
strong electromagnetic fields, it may be necessary to
be at least 60 cm (24”) away from the interfering
device to ensure proper operation. Keep the device of at least 10 cm away from any
kind of magnets. Do not connect an USB cable exceeding 3 meters
(9 feet) in length to the device. When the device is not in use, turn it off, keep the
neckloop closed and store it safely. --- Page 37 ---
Other important information
If you use several devices in the same building,
be aware that their inductive field can disturb one
another if within a range of 2 meters (6 feet). This may even occur when used in different rooms. High-powered electronic equipment, larger electronic
installations and metallic structures may impair and
significantly reduce the operating range. If the hearing aids do not respond to the device
because of an unusual field disturbance, move away
from the disturbing field. --- Page 38 ---
